title: Garlicky Quick Middle-Eastern Egg Shakshuka
description: A delicious, creamy, and garlicky Middle-Eastern dish made in one pan, perfect for a quick breakfast, lunch, or dinner. This recipe is Whole30-friendly and features dark soft brown sugar for added depth of flavor.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large cast-iron skillet over medium-low heat.
Heat the olive oil in a large cast-iron skillet over medium-low heat.
Peel and chop the onion and bell pepper, and add them to the skillet. Cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional 2 minutes, stirring constantly.
Stir in the ground cumin and cook for 1 minute.
Stir in the dark soft brown sugar and cook for 2 minutes.
Create 4 wells in the egg mixture and crack an egg into each well.
Cook the eggs for 5-7 minutes, or until the whites are set and the yolks are cooked to your desired doneness.
Garnish with chopped fresh parsley and season with salt and black pepper to taste.
